title: quercus × valachica
intro: Quercus × valachica, also known as the Valachian oak, is a hybrid plant that is a cross between the pedunculata oak (Quercus robur) and the pubescens oak (Quercus pubescens). The plant has been found to possess various medicinal properties due to its high content of flavonoids, phenolic acids, and other bioactive compounds. The health benefits associated with Quercus × valachica include its potential to lower cholesterol levels, reduce inflammation, and exhibit antioxidant properties. Quercus × valachica can be prepared as a herbal tea, tincture, or capsule, and is often used to support cardiovascular health and immune function.
